title: rhus toxicodendron subsp. diversiloba
intro: Rhus toxicodendron subsp. diversiloba, also known as poison oak, is a subspecies of the toxicodendron plant that is native to the western United States and Canada. The medicinal properties of poison oak include its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of poison oak include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of poison oak include tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
